Name: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
description: The Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_result: The "Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
detection_method: Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.
Details: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
Version used: 2025-04-15T05:54:49Z
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: An EOL version of Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unxed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.
solution: Update the Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty version on the remote host to a still supported version.

description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.

Name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-26vr-8j45-3r4w) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-26vr-8j45-3r4w) - Linux
Version used: 2024-06-11T05:05:40Z
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: When using SSL/TLS with Jetty, either with HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, or WebSocket, the server may receive an invalid large (greater than 17408) TLS frame that is incorrectly handled, causing CPU resources to eventually reach 100% usage.
solution: Update to version 9.4.39, 10.0.2, 11.0.2 or later. See the referenced vendor advisory for a possible mitigation.
insight: Aected Software/OS
Eclipse Jetty version 7.2.2 through 9.4.38, 10.0.0.alpha0 through 10.0.1 and 11.0.0.alpha0 through 11.0.1.

Name: Eclipse Jetty Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a privilege escalation vulnerability through a local temp directory.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux
Version used: 2024-06-11T05:05:40Z
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: On Unix like systems, the system's temporary directory is shared between all users on that system. A collocated user can observe the process of creating a temporary sub directory in the shared temporary directory and race to complete the creation of the temporary subdirectory. If the attacker wins the race then they will have read and write permission to the subdirectory used to unpack web applications, including their WEB-INF/lib jar les and JSP les. If any code is ever executed out of this temporary directory, this can lead to a local privilege escalation vulnerability.
insight: Aected Software/OS
Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.32.v20200930 and prior, 10.0.0.beta2 and prior and 11.0.0.beta2 and prior.

congured with only non-root contexts.

Name: Eclipse Jetty URI Parsing Vulnerability (GHSA-qh8g-58pp-2wxh) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to an URI parsing vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty URI Parsing Vulnerability (GHSA-qh8g-58pp-2wxh) - Linux OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.153234 Version used:
product_detection_result: cpe:/a:eclipse:jetty:8.1.16.20140903   Detected by Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.  ->0.800953)
impact: Aected Software/OS   Eclipse Jetty version 7.0.0 through 12.0.11.
insight: Eclipse Jetty includes a utility class, HttpURI, for URI/URL parsing.  The HttpURI class does insucient validation on the authority segment of a URI. However the  behaviour of HttpURI diers from the common browsers in how it handles a URI that would be  considered invalid if fully validated against the RRC. Specically HttpURI and the browser may  dier on the value of the host extracted from an invalid URI and thus a combination of Jetty  and a vulnerable browser may be vulnerable to a open redirect attack or to a SSRF attack if the  URI is used after passing validation checks.

Name: Eclipse Jetty Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-gwcr-j4wh-j3cq)
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability in the ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter servlet.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-gwcr-j4wh-j3cq)
Version used: 2024-06-11T05:05:40Z
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: Eclipse Jetty prior to version 9.4.41, 10.x through 10.0.2 and 11.x through 11.0.2.
insight: Requests to the ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter are able to access protected resources within the WEB-INF directory. For example a request to the ConcatServlet with a URI of /concat?/%2557EB-INF/web.xml can retrieve the web.xml le. This can reveal sensitive information regarding the implementation of a web application.

This occurs because both ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter decode the supplied path to verify it is not within the WEB-INF or META-INF directories. It then uses this decoded path to call RequestDispatcher which will also do decoding of the path. This double decoding allows paths with a doubly encoded WEB-INF to bypass this security check.

Name: Eclipse Jetty Session Vulnerability (GHSA-m6cp-vxjx-65j6) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a vulnerability in the session management.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty Session Vulnerability (GHSA-m6cp-vxjx-65j6) - Linux
Version used: 2024-06-11T05:05:40Z
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: If an exception is thrown from the SessionListener#sessionDestroyed() method, then the session ID is not invalidated in the session ID manager. On deployments with clustered sessions and multiple contexts this can result in a session not being invalidated. This can result in an application used on a shared computer being left logged in.
insight: Aected Software/OS
Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.40.v20210413 and prior, 10.x through 10.0.2 and 11.x through 11.0.2.

Name: Eclipse Jetty XXE Vulnerability (GHSA-58qw-p7qm-5rvh) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability in the XMLParser.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty XXE Vulnerability (GHSA-58qw-p7qm-5rvh) - Linux
Version used: 2024-06-11T05:05:40Z
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: XmlParser is being used when parsing Jetty's xml conguration les. An attacker might exploit this vulnerability in order to achieve SSRF or cause a denial of service.
One possible scenario is importing a (remote) malicious WAR into a Jetty's server, while the WAR includes a malicious web.xml.
insight: Aected Software/OS
Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.51 and prior, 10.0.0 through 10.0.15 and 11.0.0 through 11.0.15.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
Details: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
Version used: 2025-01-21T05:37:33Z
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
Details: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
Version used: 2025-01-21T05:37:33Z
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
detection_result: A web server is running on this port
insight: This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.50):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.
